Menu.Name: Snoot Data Sniffer
Menu.Label: Sniff Data
Menu.Hint: Open Snoot to find and clean up orphaned module data.

ShowCoreFlags.Name: Show Foundry Flags
ShowCoreFlags.Hint: Include Foundry core flags in scan results. This feature is largely untested and may produce noisy results.

ShowSystemFlags.Name: Show System Flags
ShowSystemFlags.Hint: Include game system flags in scan results. This feature is largely untested and may produce noisy results.

Disclaimer.Title: Back up your world before cleaning anything.
Disclaimer.Body: Snoot deletes settings and removes flags. There is no undo. Back up your world first.

About.Title: What Snoot does
About.Body: Snoot scans world settings and flags on every world and compendium document, groups them by module, and lets you delete data left behind by uninstalled or disabled modules.

Status.Title: Statuses
Status.System: Belongs to Foundry or your game system.
Status.Active: The relevant module is installed and enabled.
Status.Inactive: The relevant module is installed but disabled.
Status.Stale: A stored setting whose key is no longer declared by its module.

Tabs.Title: Tabs
Tabs.Overview: Per-module summary with status badges, entry counts, and clean actions. Includes total counts of orphaned and stale data.
Tabs.Settings: Every world setting in storage, grouped by namespace. Delete individual settings or every setting for a namespace.
Tabs.FlagsWorld: Every flag scope found on world documents, grouped by module.
Tabs.FlagsCompendiums: Every flag scope found on compendium documents.

Workflow.Title: Recommended workflow
Workflow.Step2: Open the Overview tab and review the orphaned and stale counts.
Workflow.Step3: Inspect what each namespace owns in the per-tab views before cleaning.
Workflow.Step4: Clean orphaned data first, then review inactive and stale entries individually.

StatusHint.system: Foundry core or game system data

CleanModule.Title: Clean Module Data
CleanModule.Content: Delete all settings and remove all flags for {module}? This cannot be undone.

CleanAll.Title: Clean All Orphaned Data
CleanAll.Content: Delete all settings and remove all flags for every orphaned module? Orphaned modules are no longer installed. This cannot be undone.

CleanInactive.Title: Clean All Inactive Data
CleanInactive.Content: Delete all settings and remove all flags for every inactive module? Inactive modules are installed but disabled. This cannot be undone.

CleanStale.Title: Clean All Stale Settings
CleanStale.Content: Delete all unregistered (stale) settings across every module? This cannot be undone.

DeleteSetting.Title: Delete Setting
DeleteSetting.Content: Delete the setting {key}? This cannot be undone.

DeleteModuleSettings.Title: Delete Module Settings
DeleteModuleSettings.Content: Delete all settings for {module}? This cannot be undone.

RemoveScope.Title: Remove Flag Scope
RemoveScope.Content: Remove all {scope} flags from every world document? This cannot be undone.

RemoveCompendiumScope.Title: Remove Compendium Flag Scope
RemoveCompendiumScope.Content: Remove all {scope} flags from every compendium document? Packs will be temporarily unlocked. This cannot be undone.

RemoveDocFlag.Title: Remove Document Flag
RemoveDocFlag.Content: Remove {scope} flags from {name}? This cannot be undone.

Tooltip.Search: Filter entries by name, key, or value
Tooltip.Stale: Key not declared by its module. Usually renamed or removed.

Card.OrphanedWorldFlags: World document flags owned by uninstalled modules
Card.OrphanedCompendiumFlags: Compendium document flags owned by uninstalled modules
Card.StaleSettings: Settings whose key is no longer declared by its module

Col.Module: Module ID that owns the data
Col.Status: Whether the owning module is installed, enabled, or missing
Col.Count: Total settings and flagged documents owned by this module
Col.Registered: Whether the key is declared by the module

Empty.Settings: No module settings found in world storage.
Empty.WorldFlags: No flag scopes found in world documents.
Empty.CompendiumFlags: No flag scopes found in compendium documents.
